Title: Dynamic auxin maxima regulate male-to-hermaphrodite conversion and de novo meristem formation in the fern Ceratopteris gametophytes

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes meristem progenitor cell establishment, cell proliferation, de novo meristem formation, and male-to-hermaphrodite conversion in fern gametophytes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Local auxin maxima at the initiation site of the new hermaphrodite body and later at the center/notch of the de novo meristem in gametophytes
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Auxin (indole-3-acetic acid) biosynthesis via the TAA1-YUC / IPyA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: TAA branch of the canonical IPyA auxin biosynthesis pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts tryptophan to indole-3-pyruvic acid in the canonical auxin biosynthesis pathway, thereby supplying substrate for downstream YUC-mediated IAA production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for local auxin biosynthesis required for de novo meristem formation and sex-type conversion
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in gametophytes at 4, 5, 8, and 10 DAG; significantly higher in hermaphrodites than males from 5 DAG onward; transcriptional reporter is localized to the meristem initiation site and later concentrated at the center/notch region of multicellular meristems; during male-to-hermaphrodite conversion, induced at the new proliferation site by 3 DAT and later confined to the center of the de novo meristem by 9 DAT
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in hermaphrodite meristem initiation site and notch center; induced at the initiation site of de novo hermaphrodite formation after transfer to antheridiogen-free medium
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCrTAA1 (native transcriptional promoter, endogenous pattern); CrU6 promoters for gRNAs in CRISPR constructs; CrUBQ10 promoter for Cas9 in editing constructs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Antheridiogen-free medium induces male-to-hermaphrodite conversion; conditioned fern medium with antheridiogen promotes male state; L-kynurenine inhibits TAA1-mediated auxin biosynthesis; exogenous IAA activates DR5 broadly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chemical inhibition with L-kynurenine; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout (crtaa1-1 and crtaa1-2); transcriptional reporter pCrTAA1::H2B-GFP::3′CrTAA1; qPCR; DR5 auxin reporter imaging; time-lapse confocal imaging; lineage tracing and division quantification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Phylogenetic/domain analysis using genome resources; no new omics accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes local auxin maxima, meristem progenitor cell lineage initiation and proliferation, de novo meristem formation, and male-to-hermaphrodite conversion; loss or inhibition blocks these processes
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of CrTAA1 prevents sex-type conversion and new meristem formation; Kyn treatment reduces growth and proliferation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CrTAA1 is the sole Ceratopteris gene within the TAA clade; other related homologs cluster with TAR3/4 and are distinct from canonical TAA1/TAR1/TAR2. Genetic knockout phenocopies Kyn inhibition, strongly supporting causality
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrTAA1 (Ceric.10G039800) is the sole canonical TAA1 homolog in Ceratopteris and belongs to the TAA clade with an Alliinase-C domain. 2. CrTAA1 expression is dynamically localized to the initiation site of de novo hermaphrodite formation and later to the center/notch of the developing meristem. 3. Chemical inhibition of TAA1 activity abolishes new auxin maxima, suppresses MPC lineage establishment and proliferation, and blocks male-to-hermaphrodite conversion. 4. CRISPR knockout alleles crtaa1-1 and crtaa1-2 phenocopy chemical inhibition, demonstrating that CrTAA1-dependent auxin biosynthesis is essential for this developmental transition.
